message:
test(aws): cover athena file:// edge-case shapes

Review-pass follow-up on c23ac82. Expands regression coverage to
explicitly verify the `athena-query-string-from-file` and
`athena-cli-input-file` patterns still match under:

- `--query-string=file://…` with `=` separator
- `--query-string "file://…"` with double-quoted file path
- `--query-string 'file://…'` with single-quoted file path
- `--query-string FILE:///…` (case-insensitive on the protocol)
- `--cli-input-json=file://…` with `=` separator

These shapes all appear in real AWS CLI invocations (shells that
default to `=`, Bash/PowerShell quoting differences, operators who
type `FILE://` out of habit). All pass — my existing regex handles
them thanks to `[=\s]+`, `['"]?`, and the leading `(?i)` flag.
